title: Satureja dentata
intro: Satureja dentata, also known as Yemeni savory, is a perennial herb belonging to the Lamiaceae family, native to the Middle East and North Africa. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory infections, and inflammation, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Satureja dentata include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Satureja dentata is commonly prepared as an essential oil, tea, or infusion, and is also used in the production of traditional remedies such as syrups and salves.
